title: Singularity AMC exits Jaro Education, promoter accumulates more shares - Moneycontrol.com
content:
Singularity was investor in Jaro Education since September 2025.
Yash Kela-founded and veteran investor Madhusudan Kela-backed Singularity AMC has exited Jaro Institute of Technology Management and Research by selling entire 1.27 percent shareholding via open market transactions on May 19.
Singularity Growth Opportunities Fund II sold 2.8 lakh shares (1.27 percent stake) in the edtech and online higher education company for Rs 14.08 crore. The selling price was Rs 501.33 per share.
Singularity was investor in Jaro Education since September 2025.
However, promoter Sanjay Namdeo Salunkhe has acquired some of those shares sold by Singularity, buying 2.09 lakh shares (0.94 percent stake) for Rs 10.5 crore at Rs 503.91 per share.
In fact, promoter has gradually been making additional buying in the company since last week. With today's stake, he acquired 2.65 percent shares in current month against promoters' shareholding of 57.28 percent as of March 2026.
Perhaps, the stock may have reacted to the deal. Jaro Education shares rallied 6.17 percent to Rs 510.80 amid high volumes on the National Stock Exchange. Overall, the stock has been in an uptrend after bottoming out last week.
Indian Emulsifiers shares were also in focus, hitting record low of Rs 50.50 on the National Stock Exchange before ending at new closing high of Rs 51.05, down 18.19 percent amid high volumes.
Siddharth Oberoi-founded portfolio management and equity research firm Prudent Equity sold 1 lakh shares (0.54 percent stake) in speciality chemicals maker Indian Emulsifiers for Rs 51.89 lakh at Rs 51.89 per share.
Meanwhile, the major action was also seen in RFBL Flexi Pack, the printed multilayer flexible packaging materials manufacturer. The stock on its listing day rallied 10.2 percent to finish at Rs 55.1 on the NSE Emerge, may be especially after 21.8 percent shares buying by several investors.
Jagid Vanitaben Rajendraprasad, L7 Securities, Smital Suresh Thakkar, Rathod Digvijaysinh Rajendrasinh, Riddhi Siddhi International, and Craft Emerging Market Fund PCC - Citadel Capital Fund have bought 50.82 lakh shares (21.79 percent stake) in RFBL for Rs 26.71 crore.
Nexus Global Opportunities Fund has bought additional 68,727 equity shares (0.98 percent stake) in Choksi Laboratories for Rs 70.82 lakh shares at Rs 103.05 per share. The fund already held 6.03 percent stake in the company as of March 2026.
On other side, CSG Financial & Management Services exited Choksi Laboratories by selling entire 70,000 shares (1 percent stake) for Rs 72.14 lakh at Rs 103.06 per share. The stock price soared 8.18 percent to Rs 107.1 on Tuesday.
